mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of future equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in falling rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 4.5%, tight labor market conditions trigger wage-push inflation expectations and Fed tightening fears, which compress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE sustains below 4.5% for three consecutive months, or the predictive power vanishes in early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: Strong manufacturing output growth combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates synchronized real and financial demand expansion, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of explicit Fed tightening, rising core inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% while CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive or unchanged when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, or the predictive power vanishes outside of rate-hiking periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher during quarters with positive GDPC1 growth compared to contraction quarters (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts cross above 1300.0 thousand units, it signals robust construction demand and consumer confidence, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as cyclical sectors rally.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when HOUST sustains above 1.3 million units for two consecutive months, or the signal fails during supply-constrained construction environments.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with accelerating price levels creates a wage-price spiral dynamic that forces aggressive monetary policy responses, ultimately suppressing equity valuations and yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered episodes, or CPIAUCSL fails to accelerate during strong PAYEMS months.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, rising industrial production outpacing consumer credit expansion indicates supply-led growth without financial overheating, which supports stable earnings and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth while rates remain below 1.5%, or the outperformance disappears when rates normalize above 2.5%.
